question: What is the mechanism by which the Windows-native Apple Music Desktop Application exposes its currently-playing track metadata (title, artist) to other Windows processes? Confirm whether the Windows.Media.Control.GlobalSystemMediaTransportControlsSessionManager (SMTC) API can be used to obtain the Title, Artist, and a MediaPropertiesChanged event for the Apple Music app, and whether the Windows Apple Music app actually publishes an SMTC session. Cite (a) Microsoft documentation for GlobalSystemMediaTransportControlsSessionManager — class existence, properties, events; (b) any canonical confirmation (Microsoft, Apple, archived community report) that the Windows Apple Music app participates in SMTC.
constraints: Target OS is Windows. The Apple Music referenced is the Apple-published Windows desktop application distributed via the Microsoft Store, NOT iTunes for Windows and NOT macOS Music.app.
Cloud / Apple Music Web API is explicitly out of scope per A7.

Findings about SMTC API capability come from Microsoft canonical docs (citation_class: static_canonical). Findings about whether the Apple Music app participates in SMTC may need archived_snapshot or interactive_doc citations from community reports if Microsoft/Apple don't directly document the participation.
If you find that Apple Music on Windows does NOT participate in SMTC, surface alternatives (window-title scraping, accessibility APIs, COM automation) with their own citation requirements.
Do not return findings as summaries of training-data familiarity. Actually fetch the Microsoft docs page and quote it.
